summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording of a funky, high‑energy show featuring a smoking Jerry and the rare Stranger / Shakedown Street combo.
review: Both reviewers rave about this performance, calling it “smokin’” and highlighting the unique inclusion of both "Stranger" and "Shakedown Street" in one set. The tape is praised for requiring no EQ at all—out‑shining other well‑known audience tapes from Bob Morris and Charlie Miller—making it an excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 5‑star reviews emphasize a stellar musical performance and an outstanding, no‑EQ‑needed recording; the limited number of reviews lowers confidence to medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sounds perfect straight out of the box—no EQ needed, crisp and balanced, judged superior to other famous audience tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Funky mood throughout; the setlist is praised as one of the reviewer’s favorites from the ’80s, with high energy and tight transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as "smokin" with a perfect tone; Mutron envelope filter highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir asked the crowd to step back, indicating his energetic presence onstage.
